master_row.title: Trawin Base Unit
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawin base unit is the invariant measure obtained by integrating the metric density around one fundamental loop that encircles the zero point exactly once. It translates the singular geometry into a reproducible unit scale rather than borrowing measurement from an external convention.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a contour integral.
master_row.dictionary: The Trawin base unit (u_T) is defined by the invariant relation (u_T:=_ g ,d) taken over a fundamental loop () encircling the TZP exactly once.
master_row.encyclopedia: The Trawin base unit $u_T$ is the fundamental geometric unit obtained by completing one metric-weighted loop around the TZP. Interpretive role: This is the registry's first explicit measurement object. Once fixed, it provides the scale used to compare time drift, action integrals, span measures, and any later normalized constants defined around the TZP.
